sections.title: Preamble
paragraphs: Enterprise KuaiShu ("we" or the "Operator") attaches great importance to the security of your personal information and privacy. These Guidelines explain how we collect, use, store, share, and protect personal information when you use Enterprise KuaiShu instant messaging and related services, and what rights you may exercise under applicable law.

We process personal information in accordance with principles of lawfulness, fairness, necessity, and good faith, and implement measures such as accountability, specified purposes, informed consent, data minimization, security safeguards, individual participation, and transparency.

Please read these Guidelines in full before registering, signing in, or using any feature. If you do not agree, please stop using the services. Continued use indicates that you have read and accepted these Guidelines.

sections.title: 1. Collection and Use of Personal Information
paragraphs: Personal information means any information relating to an identified or identifiable natural person recorded electronically or otherwise, excluding information that has been anonymized.

subSections.subtitle: 1.1 Categories of information we may collect
paragraphs: (1) Registration and sign-in data: When you create or access an Enterprise KuaiShu account, we may collect your mobile number, email address, employee ID, organization or department, credentials, and information needed for identity verification and tenant isolation.

(2) Identity and compliance data: To protect accounts and meet legal or corporate requirements, we may collect real-name verification materials, employment proof, or other information required by law or your organization.

(3) Communication and collaboration data: When you use messaging, groups, contacts, file transfer, audio/video calls, or workflow features, we may process chat content, contact relationships, group membership, call metadata, uploaded or downloaded files, and operation logs needed to deliver core services.

(4) Device and log data: To maintain stability and security, we may collect device model, OS version, app version, network type, IP address, device identifiers, crash logs, performance metrics, and security audit logs.

(5) Optional permission-based data: If you grant camera, microphone, photo library, contacts, or location permissions, we may collect related information for the features you enable; refusing a permission usually affects only that feature.

subSections.subtitle: 1.2 Purposes of use
paragraphs: (1) Providing instant messaging, organizational collaboration, file sharing, conferencing, and enterprise management capabilities;
(2) Identity verification, account protection, abnormal sign-in detection, anti-fraud, and illegal content governance;
(3) Troubleshooting, performance optimization, product improvement, and anonymized statistical analysis;
(4) Responding to inquiries, complaints, and rights requests, and sending necessary service-related notices;
(5) Fulfilling legal obligations and cooperating with regulators or judicial authorities as required by law.

sections.title: 2. Cookies and Similar Technologies
paragraphs: To keep clients, admin consoles, and related web services running properly, we may store cookies, local cache, or similar identifiers on your device to maintain sign-in state, remember language preferences, measure visits, and improve loading performance.

We do not use these technologies for purposes beyond those described here. You may clear, restrict, or refuse cookies through browser or system settings; some features may then be unavailable, and you bear the resulting impact.

subSections.subtitle: 3.1 Sharing
paragraphs: We do not sell your personal information to third parties outside the Enterprise KuaiShu Operator. We may share necessary information only when:
(1) We have obtained your separate consent or another lawful basis applies;
(2) Required to comply with laws, legal process, or mandatory requests from authorities;
(3) Reasonably necessary to protect the rights, property, or safety of Enterprise KuaiShu, users, or the public;
(4) Shared with affiliates for unified accounts, risk control, or group deployment, subject to these Guidelines;
(5) Shared with processors such as cloud, SMS, push, customer service, or security vendors under contractual confidentiality and security obligations.

subSections.subtitle: 3.2 Transfer
paragraphs: We will not transfer your personal information to any company, organization, or individual except:
(1) With your explicit consent; or
(2) In mergers, divisions, dissolution, bankruptcy, or asset transfers, where the recipient must continue to be bound by these Guidelines or obtain your consent again.

subSections.subtitle: 3.3 Public disclosure
paragraphs: We publicly disclose personal information only when:
(1) You have given explicit consent or made an active choice; or
(2) Disclosure is required by laws, litigation, arbitration, or competent authorities.

sections.title: 4. Storage and Protection
paragraphs: (1) We apply encryption in transit, access control, least privilege, security auditing, and vulnerability remediation to reduce unauthorized access, leakage, alteration, or loss.
(2) We collect only what is necessary and retain data no longer than required unless law or your consent provides otherwise.
(3) We maintain internal policies and incident response plans and train staff with access to personal information.
(4) No method of transmission or storage is completely secure; please safeguard your account credentials.

sections.title: 5. Your Rights
paragraphs: Subject to applicable laws in China, you may exercise the following rights regarding your personal information when statutory conditions are met:

subSections.subtitle: 5.1 Access and copy
paragraphs: You may request access to or a copy of personal information we hold about you, except where law provides otherwise, via in-app settings or your enterprise administrator.

subSections.subtitle: 5.2 Rectification
paragraphs: If you find inaccuracies or incompleteness, you may request correction or supplementation after we verify your identity.

subSections.subtitle: 5.3 Deletion
paragraphs: You may request deletion when:
(1) The processing purpose is achieved, impossible, or no longer necessary;
(2) We cease providing products or services, or the retention period expires;
(3) You withdraw consent and no other lawful basis exists;
(4) We process personal information in violation of law or our agreement with you.

subSections.subtitle: 5.4 Withdraw consent and account cancellation
paragraphs: You may withdraw consent for non-essential processing at any time; some features may then be unavailable. You may also request account cancellation under enterprise rules; we will delete or anonymize data after verification as required by law.

sections.title: 6. Children's Personal Information
paragraphs: Enterprise KuaiShu is primarily intended for enterprise and organizational users and generally does not offer independent registration to children under fourteen.
If processing children's information is necessary, we will obtain guardian consent as required by law and use or disclose data only to the extent needed to protect the child.

sections.title: 7. Cross-border Provision
paragraphs: In principle, personal information collected and generated within the People's Republic of China is stored on servers located in China.

If cross-border transfer is required, we will comply with security assessment, standard contracts, certification, or other legal procedures and obtain your separate consent unless law provides otherwise.

sections.title: 8. Updates to These Guidelines
paragraphs: We may revise these Guidelines from time to time and notify you through in-app notices, pop-ups, email, or other reasonable means.

Without your explicit consent, we will not reduce your rights under these Guidelines. For material changes, we will provide more prominent notice; if you disagree, you may stop using the services; continued use constitutes acceptance.

sections.title: 9. Third-party Services and Risk Notice
paragraphs: Enterprise KuaiShu may integrate third-party maps, push, cloud storage, or identity services governed by their own privacy policies; please review them carefully.
We will assist within reasonable limits for outages caused by networks, devices, third parties, or force majeure, but are not liable beyond what law permits for events not attributable to us.
You are responsible for the legality of information you send to other users or external systems through Enterprise KuaiShu.

sections.title: 10. Contact Us
paragraphs: For questions, comments, or complaints about these Guidelines or our processing practices, contact us through in-app feedback or your enterprise administrator; we will respond within a reasonable period.
Interpretation of these Guidelines rests with the Enterprise KuaiShu Operator to the extent permitted by law.

sections.title: 1. Acceptance and Effect
paragraphs: Welcome to Enterprise KuaiShu! This Service Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into between you and the Enterprise KuaiShu Operator and governs your download, installation, registration, sign-in, and use of clients, admin tools, and related services.

Please read all terms carefully before accepting or using the services, especially provisions on limitation of liability, disclaimers, and dispute resolution. By accepting, registering, or continuing to use the services, you agree to be bound by this Agreement.
If you use the services on behalf of an enterprise or organization, you represent that you are duly authorized and that this Agreement binds that entity as well.

sections.title: 2. Service Description
paragraphs: Enterprise KuaiShu provides secure, efficient instant messaging and collaboration for enterprises and organizations, including one-to-one and group chat, file transfer, audio/video calls, directory sync, multi-device sign-in, and integration with enterprise systems (subject to available features).
We may adjust, optimize, or discontinue modules for upgrades, compliance, or operations and will notify you by reasonable means unless law requires otherwise.
Certain advanced capabilities may require separate activation or fees as shown in the product or a separate commercial contract.

sections.title: 3. Account Registration and Security
paragraphs: 3.1 You must provide true, accurate, complete, and lawful information and keep it updated; losses from inaccurate information are your responsibility.
3.2 You are responsible for safeguarding credentials and bound devices; actions under your account are deemed yours. Report suspected compromise promptly to us or your administrator.
3.3 You agree to receive necessary system, security, and update notices; marketing messages require consent where required by law.

sections.title: 4. User Conduct
paragraphs: 4.1 You must comply with applicable laws, regulations, and your organization's internal policies.
4.2 You must not use the services for fraud, gambling, pyramid schemes, money laundering, national security violations, rumor spreading, or other illegal activities.
4.3 You must not publish or transmit obscene, violent, terrorist, defamatory, privacy-invasive, infringing, or otherwise unlawful content.
4.4 You must not access, interfere with, or attack servers or networks, or distribute viruses, malware, or harmful scripts.
4.5 You must not send spam at scale, scrape data without authorization, or harm other users or third parties.

sections.title: 5. Personal Information Protection
paragraphs: 5.1 We protect personal information as described in the Enterprise KuaiShu Privacy Guidelines and will not disclose it to unrelated third parties without consent unless required by law.
5.2 We apply reasonable technical and organizational measures; you should use strong passwords and enterprise security policies.
5.3 We may collect device, log, and user-submitted information as necessary to provide and improve services, as detailed in the Privacy Guidelines.

sections.title: 6. Intellectual Property
paragraphs: 6.1 Software, UI, documentation, trademarks, and related technology belong to the Operator or rights holders; you may not copy, modify, distribute, or commercialize them without written permission.
6.2 You may not reverse engineer, disassemble, or decompile the software except where expressly permitted by law.
6.3 You retain rights in content you upload or send but grant the Operator a worldwide, royalty-free, non-exclusive license to use it for provision, backup, display, and compliance review.

sections.title: 7. Disclaimers and Limitation of Liability
paragraphs: 7.1 To the maximum extent permitted by law, we are not liable beyond statutory limits for interruptions, delays, or data loss caused by networks, third parties, device failures, or force majeure, though we will endeavor to restore services.
7.2 Services are provided "as is" and "as available" without warranties of uninterrupted, error-free, or fit-for-purpose operation for your specific business goals.
7.3 You bear responsibility for violations of this Agreement, corporate rules, or law, and must compensate us or third parties for resulting losses.

sections.title: 8. Changes, Suspension, and Termination
paragraphs: 8.1 We may temporarily suspend services for maintenance, security, upgrades, or business adjustments with advance notice when practicable.
8.2 We may warn, restrict, freeze, or terminate accounts for serious breaches or threats to platform security and reserve legal remedies.
8.3 You may deactivate or cancel accounts under enterprise rules; we will delete or anonymize personal information as required by law after termination.

sections.title: 9. Amendments
paragraphs: We may amend this Agreement for legal or business reasons and publish updates in the app or on official channels. If you disagree, stop using the services; continued use constitutes acceptance.

sections.title: 10. Governing Law and Disputes
paragraphs: 10.1 This Agreement is governed by the laws of the mainland People's Republic of China (excluding conflict-of-law rules).
10.2 Disputes should first be resolved through negotiation; failing that, either party may bring suit in a competent court where the Operator is domiciled.

sections.title: 11. Third-party Technology
paragraphs: Enterprise KuaiShu may integrate third-party SDKs, cloud, push, or map services; you must also comply with third-party terms.
We will reasonably assist with third-party failures but are not jointly liable for third-party fault except as required by law.
Install official releases promptly; risks from unofficial or tampered builds are yours.

sections.title: 12. Miscellaneous
paragraphs: If any provision is invalid, the remainder remains effective.
Matters not covered here may be governed by the Privacy Guidelines, product documentation, or separate written contracts; stricter protections for you prevail where law allows.
Final interpretation of this Agreement rests with the Enterprise KuaiShu Operator within legal limits.
